reasoning: The market probability of a formal ceasefire by March 31 was 17% on March 6, but typical time decay combined with unbridgeable diplomatic gaps (US demands for nuclear termination vs Iran's demands for reparations) pushes this much lower over the remaining 16 days. A summary of the market page indicates a high-volume trader estimated the probability at 3-4% due to these massive gaps, while related scenarios show high likelihoods for ceasefires only by late April or June. The short remaining timeline and severe ongoing deadlock drive the estimate down to 5%.
